summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve program operations—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Own {prog|end-to-end} {coord|onboarding} workflows for 20+ concurrent programs, maintaining data integrity in tracking systems via feedback loops
Serve as primary liaison across {cross|Marketing, Tech, and Sales} and 10+ vendors, reconciling records and refining delivery strategy
Build documentation and digital resource libraries for 70+ staff, streamlining operations and cutting onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate listening sessions with 25 global {cross|stakeholders}, translating feedback into 3 new programs and a 78% enrollment rate
Facilitate CEO/HR discovery and stakeholder interviews to diagnose operational drivers and inform evidence-based process redesigns
Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to resolve cross-functional process and accountability gaps
Built weekly {data|reporting} by transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed engagement and program strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights and recommending actions to the C-suite on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|program experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Optimized {coord|onboarding} processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Directed a Merger & Acquisition project, migrating and merging employee records across systems to maintain data integrity
Streamlined immigration and relocation processes through close coordination with attorneys and {cross|cross-functional} teams
Piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

projects: Consulting Projects: Informed Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution

Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Program & PM Tools: Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · Knowledge Management (SharePoint, Notion) · CRM (HubSpot)
Data & Systems: Data Analysis (Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS) · HRIS/SaaS Admin (SAP, Rippling, ADP) · ATS (Greenhouse)

jd.meta: Anthropic — Program Operations Manager, Human Data

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Anthropic — Program Operations Manager, Human Data. Location → San Francisco, CA (role base; also NYC). Summary → Strategic Program Manager, program-operations flavor. InGenius → Program Operations Manager title + Set F: end-to-end onboarding workflows/lifecycles + data integrity, primary liaison across teams/vendors + record reconciliation, documentation, listening sessions + 78% enrollment. Reordered InGenius to lead. ODN → Pair B (diagnostic). NextGen → weekly reporting framing. Vestas → onboarding/process + M&A record migration (data integrity). Skills → dropped HRCI/LMS, led with PM + KM tools, added Claude. Maps cleanly to onboarding/access/vendor/reporting JD.
